ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON NOV-29-2017 IN 503, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF. GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE JULY 16, 2018 TRIAL DATE IS ADVANCED TO MARCH 19, 2018 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO CCP 36(F) IS MAR-29-2018. LOCAL RULE 20 SHALL BE FOLLOWED BY THE PARTIES AS WELL AS ALL SFLR AND CRC. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS MAR-15-2018. SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS SHALL BE BROUGHT ON REGULAR NOTICE PURSUANT TO THE RELEVANT PROVISIONS OF THE CCP, UNLESS THE PARTIES STIPULATE OTHERWISE. THE COURT WILL ALLOW A MAXIMUM OF FOUR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS PER DAY TO BE CALENDARED IN THIS ACTION. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 9:30A.M. ON MARCH 15, 2018. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JANUARY 30, 2018 AT 1:30P.M. NO NEED FOR ANY PARTY TO FILE ANY CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENT. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO CRC, RULE 3.1380 AND SFLR OF COURT, RULES 5.0 AND 20.2 ON MARCH 19, 2018 AT 1:30P.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE PARTIES SHALL COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULES 5.0 AND 20.2 AND PROVIDE A CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT STATEMENT BY MARCH 15, 2018 AT 2:00P.M. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, MATTER NOT REPORTED

Plaintiff'S Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Add To Calendar For Issuing Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Nov-29-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancingt The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Nov-29-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party John Liptrot
Alex Alpert of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendants General Electric Company, CBS Corporation and Thomas Dee Engineering Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No objections made. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to advance the trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The July 16, 2018 trial date is advanced to March 19, 2018 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is March 29, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 15, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 2, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 16, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by March 12, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on March 15, 2018. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 2, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 16, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 29, 2017. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than December 13, 2017 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than December 13, 2017 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 30, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on March 19, 2018 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by March 15, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported. (503/CML)
